question: Write a C++ program that prompts the user to enter the length and width of a rectangle (as floating-point numbers), then calculates and displays its area and perimeter with appropriate labels.

test_cases.expected:
Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Calculation Results ---
Area: 22.50
Perimeter: 21.00

Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Calculation Results ---
Area: 1.00
Perimeter: 4.00

Enter the length of the rectangle: Enter the width of the rectangle: 
--- Calculation Results ---
Area: 69.97
Perimeter: 36.02

driver: int main() { float l, w; std::cout << "Enter the length of the rectangle: "; std::cin >> l; std::cout << "Enter the width of the rectangle: "; std::cin >> w; float a = l * w; float p = l + w + l + w; std::cout << "\n--- Calculation Results ---\n"; std::cout << "Area: " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << a << std::endl; std::cout << "Perimeter: " << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << p << std::endl; return 0; }
errors: Poorly chosen variable names (e.g., l, w, a, p) reduce readability
Perimeter calculation is less efficient and readable (l+w+l+w instead of 2*(l+w))
Lack of comments explaining the purpose of variables or complex logic
